meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no testing knowledge (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no testing knowledge. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Advanced User Onboarding: Solving Testing Gaps for Students
content.seo_description: Master user onboarding to solve testing knowledge gaps. A high-level guide for AI students using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade apps.
content.answer_block: You lack testing knowledge because you treat code as a static script rather than a dynamic journey. By implementing robust user onboarding, you force yourself to build modular, state-aware flows. This forces automated testing into your architecture, turning your 'no-testing' habit into a rigorous, professional-grade development workflow.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect AI feature, but your terminal is a graveyard of silent errors and unhandled edge cases. You feel the crushing weight of 'no-testing knowledge'—that invisible wall between a student project and a production-ready SaaS. Every time you push to main, you’re playing a high-stakes game of Russian roulette with your user experience.

But here is the secret: user onboarding isn't just about UI; it’s an architectural blueprint for stability. By forcing your application to guide a user from zero to 'aha moment,' you are compelled to validate every state, every failure, and every success. We are going to bridge that gap, leveraging the sheer force of Antigravity and Claude Code to automate your testing trajectory.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha' State Machine
steps.description: Before writing code, map the user journey as a finite state machine. This forces you to define what 'success' looks like, which is the prerequisite for writing your first unit test.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a state machine diagram for a SaaS onboarding flow. Define transitions for user registration, onboarding completion, and error states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a state machine typescript interface for user onboarding'

steps.title: Implement Test-Driven Onboarding Components
steps.description: Build your onboarding modals as isolated components. By focusing on the component interface, you naturally create units that are easy to test in isolation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React component for a user onboarding step that accepts an 'onComplete' callback and handles loading states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create unit tests for the onboarding component using Vitest'

steps.title: Automate User Path Integration
steps.description: Connect your onboarding flow to your backend API. Use this as your first integration test suite to ensure the frontend and backend speak the same language.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write an integration test script that simulates a user moving through the onboarding flow and hitting the backend API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add integration tests for the onboarding API endpoints'

steps.title: Simulate Failure States
steps.description: Professional apps don't just work; they handle failure gracefully. Force your onboarding to fail intentionally to test your error boundaries.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of edge-case scenarios for onboarding including network timeouts and invalid user inputs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate mock data to test onboarding failure states'

steps.title: Continuous Deployment Validation
steps.description: Automate the execution of your onboarding tests every time you push code. This is how you move from a student to a professional engineer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CI/CD configuration file that runs the onboarding test suite on every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure github actions to run tests on push'

pro_tips: Treat every UI state as an opportunity to write a unit test; if you can't test it, your UI is too complex.
Use mock service workers (MSW) to intercept API calls during onboarding tests so you don't hit your production database.
Focus on testing the happy path first, then use Claude Code to generate 'chaos' tests for user abandonment.
Document your onboarding flow with ADRs (Architecture Decision Records) to keep your project professional.
Never ship a feature without a corresponding test file; if the test doesn't exist, the feature doesn't exist.
content.next_steps: Now that your onboarding is rock-solid and tested, move to implementing an automated feedback loop where user onboarding data informs your next product iteration.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the professional mindset to stop guessing and start building. Don't let a lack of testing hold your brilliance back from the market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
